description: Global hallucination audit for halley-labs/. Flags fabricated results, simulated benchmarks, unverifiable claims, broken references, and missing evidence across all 27 repos.
methodology: 1. Cross-referenced every empirical claim in groundings.json against real_benchmark_results.json data
2. Scanned all sota_benchmark.py scripts for random metric assignment, simulate comments, placeholder values
3. Verified all 511 citation DOIs for format validity and year consistency
4. Verified all code_references point to existing files on disk
5. Checked all 4 tool_paper.tex files for ungrounded theorems/definitions
6. Detected sentinel values (999.0) indicating benchmark infrastructure failures
7. Detected error entries in benchmark results indicating runtime crashes

flagged_claims.statement: For schema-only perturbations, ARC achieves zero cost (ratio 0.00) via algebraic annihilation; all baselines require full recomputation (ratios ≥ 0.92).
issues: Claim that 'all baselines require full recomputation (ratios >= 0.92)' includes brute-force baseline with simulated timing (time.sleep at L555). Alembic-DDL baseline performs real DataFrame operations.

flagged_claims.statement: For compound perturbations, ARC achieves cost ratio 0.002 while all baselines have ratio ≥ 0.92, giving a 575× improvement.
issues: The 575x improvement figure includes comparison to brute-force baseline which uses time.sleep simulation (L555), not actual pipeline re-execution.
Result was simulated, not measured

flagged_claims.statement: ARC delivers 3.6× faster repair than Alembic-DDL (1.02ms vs 3.72ms), and 78× faster than brute-force (1.02ms vs 79.78ms), across 500 realistic pipeline repair tests.
issues: Brute-force baseline timing uses time.sleep(0.001*len/1000) at L555, not real re-execution. The 78x speedup vs brute-force is fabricated. ARC vs Alembic-DDL comparison (3.6x) uses real DataFrame operations.
Result was simulated, not measured

flagged_claims.statement: Real-world benchmark: 15 market regimes, 6 assets, mean shield improvement 70.3% (dominated by COVID-crash outlier).
issues: Benchmark results include regime_detection_accuracy=0.7 as a hardcoded placeholder (L510), not an actual measurement of regime detection performance. Trading metrics (returns, Sharpe) are computed independently and not affected by this placeholder.

flagged_claims.statement: COVID-19 crash: Momentum+Shield Sharpe=4.18 vs Momentum Sharpe=-0.43, shield activation 35%.
issues: Benchmark results for shield strategies include placeholder regime_detection_accuracy=0.7 (L510). Shield activation rate and trading metrics are computed from actual strategy logic, not from this placeholder.

flagged_claims.statement: All baselines (differential testing naive 10K, UB-aware, IR comparison) detect 0/30 UB divergences.
issues: Differential testing baseline uses random.random() to simulate results (L878-881). LLVM IR baseline uses random.random() (L959-961). Neither baseline performs actual compilation, execution, or IR analysis. The '0/30 detection' result is from random simulation, not real differential testing.
Result was simulated, not measured

flagged_claims.statement: On 10 controlled pairs (Table 5): differential testing 100% accuracy, SemRec 70% accuracy; each finds 1 unique divergence.
issues: Differential testing baseline results are from random.random() simulation (L873-881), not actual compilation and execution of C/Rust code pairs. The '100% accuracy' figure is a random-seed-dependent outcome of simulated testing.
Result was simulated, not measured

flagged_claims.statement: Ablation study (Table 9): Full pipeline 80% accuracy, no σ-bridge (plain BV) 40%, random testing 10K 80%.
issues: Ablation study may include comparisons to simulated baselines (differential testing at L858-881 uses random.random()). Main pipeline results appear to be from actual tool execution.
